notes: Bezaleel "in the shadow or protection of God". He belonged to the tribe of Judah and the grandson of Hur (Ex 17:12), who was called with to lead the people during the absence of Moses. According to Jewish tradition, Hur was the husband of Miriam.

quotes.text: In cutting of stones, etc.— Every thing that concerned the lapidary’s, jeweller’s, and carver’s art.
quotes.speaker: Adam Clarke

notes: Aholiab " Father's tent". He was of the tribe of Dan. Ultimately Israel would be designated as "Dan to Beersheba". (see Judges 20:1; 1 Samuel 3:20; 2 Samuel 3:10; 1 Kings 4:25) Beersheba is on the southernmost border of Judah's territory. The Lord is calling two individuals to supervise a tabernacle (the father's tent 'Aholiab') that will govern the entire tribes 'from Dan to Beersheba'.
in the hearts of all that are wise hearted I have put wisdom, that they may make all that I have commanded thee;

Elder Bruce R. Mc Conkie taught: "Sabbath worship … which singles out one day in seven to be used exclusively for spiritual things, is a sign which identifies the Lord's people…. True religion always has and always will call for a Sabbath on which men rest from their temporal labors and work exclusively on spiritual matters…. Without a Sabbath of rest and worship, men's hearts will never be centered on the things of the Spirit sufficiently to assure them of salvation" (The Promised Messiah [Salt Lake City: Deseret Book Co., 1978], 390).

quotes.text: The token or sign of the great covenant with all Israel made at Sinai was the Sabbath. (See Exodus 31:12-17.)
quotes.speaker: Howard W. Hunter
quotes.attr: Howard W. Hunter, in Conference Report, Ensign, Nov. 1982, 57

notes: Elder Russell M. Nelson explained: "Now I understand that my behavior on the Sabbath is my sign to the Lord of my regard [respect and love] for him and for the covenant under which I was born. If … my interests on the Sabbath were turned to pro football games or worldly movies, the sign from me to him would clearly be that my devotion would not favor the Lord. If, on the other hand, my Sabbath interests were focused on the Lord and his teachings, my family, or the sick, or the poor, and the needy, that sign would likewise be visible to God" (The Power within Us [Salt Lake City: Deseret Book Co., 1988], 127).
